positioning.oneLiner: Enhancify is a contractor financing technology platform that helps businesses offer homeowner financing options without dealer fees.
positioning.primaryOutcome: Help contractors win more jobs, increase average job size, reduce dealer-fee drag, and offer financing without becoming the lender.
positioning.category: Home improvement and home services financing marketplace for contractors.
differentiators: Marketplace model with roughly 30 lending institutions instead of a single direct lender.
No dealer fees charged to contractors on financed projects.
Co-branded contractor financing portal, contractor dashboard, marketing assets, and sales training.
Soft-pull pre-approval flow before the homeowner selects an option and completes final lender approval.
Designed for newer and smaller contractors that may not meet direct-lender business requirements.

salesMotion.leadSource: Warm inbound leads from contractors who expressed interest in offering financing or comparing options.
salesMotion.primaryChannel: Phone-first conversations; most sales conversations are phone only.
salesMotion.averageContractValue: Approximately $1,500 per year, with standard annual subscriptions ranging from $1,249 to $4,999.
salesMotion.targetClosePattern: One-call close when fit and urgency are present; overall sales cycle is normally under two weeks.
salesMotion.benchmarkConversion: About 25% of leads close in the current motion.
salesMotion.coachBias: Move quickly from permission to segment ID, quantified pain, value math, expectation-setting, and a clear close without enterprise-style over-discovery.

bestFit: Residential home improvement or home service contractors such as roofing, remodeling, fencing, landscaping, windows, siding, pools, spas, and similar project-based services.
Average job size generally between $5,000 and $50,000, with a strong sweet spot around $10,000 to $20,000 projects.
Projects are normally scheduled at least one week after estimate or sale, giving financing time to fit the project timeline.
Contractors who lose deals, shrink project scope, discount, or absorb margin when homeowners react to price.
Contractors paying for marketing or working valuable referral leads where each missed estimate has clear opportunity cost.
Contractors currently using dealer-fee financing and feeling margin pressure, approval friction, or cash-flow constraints.
poorFit: Solar dealers whose pitch depends on replacing an electric bill with a very low financing payment through heavy dealer-fee buy-downs.
Emergency or same-day/next-day HVAC install motions where the homeowner needs immediate funding and installation before a typical funding window can work.
Commercial-only contractors or customers that require commercial lending as the main use case.
Custom home builders or very large project sellers where the project economics do not match the platform's typical financing patterns.
Contractors who require guaranteed contractor-direct funding or cannot accept that funds go to the homeowner/applicant.
Prospects who need guaranteed rates, guaranteed approvals, guaranteed loan amounts, or guaranteed terms.

priorities: Stop losing jobs to price objections or competitors with financing.
Look professional without adding complex back-office work.
Get a simple tool they can use from the truck, jobsite, or estimate.
pains: The owner is constantly on the move and does not want a complicated process.
Lost jobs are personal because the owner generated, sold, and often performs the work.
They are cautious about wasting hard-earned money on another subscription.
commonObjections: Will my customers actually get approved?
I need to think about it or talk to my spouse/partner.

priorities: Scale average ticket and close rate.
Train the team to offer financing consistently.
pains: Dealer fees can force price increases or reduce profit margin.
Low approvals or funding timing can stall jobs.
Growth goals require better conversion from existing estimate volume.
commonObjections: How is this different from my current lender?
Does the money come to me or the customer?
What support do I get after I sign up?

triggerEvents: A homeowner asks about financing or monthly payments.
A contractor loses estimates after presenting price.
The contractor starts paying for marketing and needs more estimate conversion.
A contractor pays 7-15% dealer fees or raises prices to offset them.
A direct lender denies too many applicants or requires business history/revenue the contractor cannot meet.
The contractor wants financing available before the busy season.

primarySegments.name: Contractor A - No Financing Solution
primarySegments.definition: The contractor does not currently offer financing and is trying to solve price objections, lost deals, smaller project scopes, or growth constraints.
diagnosticSignals: Homeowners ask about financing or monthly payments.
Prospect loses jobs after price is presented.
Prospect discounts, reduces scope, or waits on customer callbacks.
Prospect has revenue or hiring goals that require better estimate conversion.
bestQuestions: Out of your last 10 estimates, how many turned into price objections or financing conversations?
When someone cannot move forward at the full project price, do they disappear, delay, or shrink the job?
What is your average job size, and what would one extra closed job per month be worth?
Are those leads paid, referral, or repeat customer opportunities?
primarySegments.solutionAngle: Show how a monthly-payment option can help preserve price, win more jobs, and create upsell room without making the contractor become a financing expert.

primarySegments.name: Contractor B - Dealer-Fee or Direct-Lender Financing
primarySegments.definition: The contractor already uses a financing option and is feeling dealer-fee cost, strict credit boxes, denial friction, or funding/process mismatch.
diagnosticSignals: Prospect pays 7-15% dealer fees on financed jobs.
Prospect raises prices or eats margin to use financing.
Prospect sees denials from strict direct-lender requirements.
Prospect dislikes rate sheets, buy-down complexity, or post-completion funding constraints.
bestQuestions: What percent of your projects do you finance today?
What dealer fee are you usually paying, and on what average financed job size?
Do you usually eat that fee or build it into the price?
How often do applicants get denied or fail to get an option that works?
primarySegments.solutionAngle: Position Enhancify as a marketplace alternative that can eliminate dealer-fee drag, expand options, and convert financed jobs into a cash-like workflow for the contractor.

poorFitSegments.reason: Solar economics often depend on 20-30% dealer-fee buy-downs to create a payment that replaces the electric bill; Enhancify does not enable that same pitch.
Same-day or next-day emergency installs may not fit normal funding timing, especially where the job must start immediately.
Enhancify does not guarantee direct-to-contractor funding; the applicant receives funds directly.
The platform cannot guarantee approvals, rates, loan amounts, terms, or funding timing.

offers.name: Self-Service Plan
bestFor: Contractors who mainly need the financing tech, co-branded page, and core tools.
Owners comfortable implementing financing with lighter support.
problemsSolved: Gives the contractor a financing link, calculator, marketing materials, customer support, commission eligibility, and a branded page.
Creates a low-cost annual entry point for offering financing without dealer fees.
notFor: Contractors who need hands-on training or frequent support from a dedicated account manager.
Contractors who want Enhancify specialists helping homeowners through the process.
offers.deliveryNotes: Annual plan. Public site frames this as less than $104/month.

offers.name: Professional Growth Plan
bestFor: Contractors who want expert training, onboarding, and a stronger support layer.
The default recommendation when the buyer wants ROI but still expects help getting financing adopted.
problemsSolved: Includes Self-Service capabilities plus personal training, onboarding, meetings or calls on request, same-day response expectations, dedicated account manager, and up to 5 team members.
Helps the contractor implement financing into their sales process early enough to create renewal-worthy usage.
notFor: Contractors who only need the tech and will not use training or support.
Contractors who want Enhancify to handle homeowner financing conversations directly.
offers.deliveryNotes: Annual plan. Public site frames this as less than $146/month and marks it as popular.

offers.name: Concierge Plan
bestFor: Contractors who want finance experts to help homeowners through the financing process.
Teams where customer hand-holding and response speed are more valuable than the lowest subscription price.
problemsSolved: Includes broader support, unlimited customized branded pages, dedicated financial specialist support for customers, quick response times, personalized customer assistance, and up to 10 team members.
Acts like adding financing support capacity without hiring an internal finance specialist.
notFor: Price-sensitive contractors who are unlikely to use customer-assist services.
Prospects whose average job size or estimate volume cannot justify the premium tier.
offers.deliveryNotes: Annual plan. Public site frames this as less than $417/month. Internal loop docs note legacy Concierge promo variants may exist when explicitly approved.

plans.name: Self-Service
plans.positioning: For contractors that just need the tech and will do the rest.

plans.name: Professional Growth
plans.positioning: For contractors that want expert training and are ready to grow immediately.

plans.name: Concierge
plans.positioning: For contractors who want finance experts to hold their customers' hand through the financing process.

commissions: Contractors can earn a minimum $50 commission per funded project on eligible tiers.
Avoid promising a percentage commission rate or implying most loans create large commission payouts.
Concierge commission rules may differ from Self-Service and Professional Growth; verify current policy before promising.
guarantees: Results Guarantee: summarized from training docs as a first-30-days refund policy tied to reviewing training materials and submitting ten customer applications without successful project funding.
Do not describe the guarantee loosely; use current approved policy language when closing.

promotions.name: $250 Commission Promotion
promotions.eligibility: Professional Growth Plan only, first two qualifying deals within the first two months, when currently approved.
promotions.useCase: Create urgency and encourage early implementation.

promotions.name: 10% First-Year Discount
promotions.useCase: Trade a first-year discount for timely close commitment.

promotions.name: Short Buyer's Remorse Refund Period
promotions.eligibility: Use selectively when approved and time-boxed.
promotions.useCase: Help a hesitant but qualified buyer confront a decision without creating an abuse path.

closeRequirements: Confirm the lead is qualified and sees a painful business problem.
Set proper expectations about product limits before collecting payment.
Cover the Results Guarantee policy using approved language.
Confirm primary contact information before payment.
Send the transition to success email and complete new-client notes after close.

mustHave: Fits residential contractor or home services ICP, or a clearly analogous high-ticket service business.
Has a painful business problem tied to financing, price objection, job size, close rate, dealer fees, approvals, or funding timing.
Can afford an annual subscription and understands the cost of not solving the issue.
Can use a homeowner/applicant-funded model where the customer receives funds directly.
Has enough urgency to make a decision now or accept a concrete follow-up path.
strongSignals: Projects are usually scheduled at least a week out.
The contractor pays for leads or has valuable referral leads they do not want to waste.
The contractor has lost jobs because customers could not pay cash or objected to price.
The contractor already pays dealer fees or sees denials with a direct lender.
redFlags: Solar pitch depends on very low monthly payment through heavy buy-downs.
Emergency same-day install is the primary motion.
The prospect demands guaranteed approvals, rates, terms, loan amounts, or direct-to-contractor funding.
The prospect cannot articulate a current problem or urgent reason to change.
The prospect only wants the cheapest possible subscription and rejects implementation work.
discoveryQuestions: What got you interested in offering financing right now?
Have you ever used a financing option before?
What is your average job size?
What is the typical time from estimate to project start?
Out of 10 estimates, how many mention financing, monthly payments, or price concerns?
When price becomes the issue, do you lose the job, shrink the scope, discount, or wait on a callback?
If you already finance jobs, what dealer fee are you paying and how often are you using it?
What would one extra closed job per month be worth?
What growth goal makes solving this worth doing now?
qualifiedOpportunityChecklist: The prospect is aware of a painful problem.
The rep and prospect agree on root cause, impact, and current vs desired situation.
Status quo is not comfortable or consequence-free.
The prospect understands price, alternatives, and cost of inaction.

stages.name: Opening and Permission
stages.goal: Establish sharp, enthusiastic, expert presence and get permission to ask questions before explaining product.
stages.coachInstruction: Use the inbound context, confirm owner/decision-maker status, ask what got them interested, then request permission to see if Enhancify can help.

stages.name: Fast Qualification and Contractor A/B Branch
stages.goal: Identify no-financing, dealer-fee financing, or poor-fit path quickly.
stages.coachInstruction: Ask whether they currently offer financing and, if yes, how it works and what it costs them.

stages.name: Quantified Discovery
stages.goal: Uncover current situation, frequency, financial impact, collateral damage, and desired situation.
stages.coachInstruction: Ask fewer, better questions. Quantify lost jobs, reduced project size, dealer fees, denial rate, and the value of one or two additional wins.

stages.name: Solution Mapped to Pain
stages.goal: Connect Enhancify to the exact problem already agreed on.
stages.coachInstruction: Explain only the pieces that solve the prospect's pain: branded page, marketplace, soft-pull pre-approval, no dealer fees, support/training, and relevant plan.

stages.name: Expectation-Setting and Close
stages.goal: Set product boundaries, ask clearly for the business, handle the first no, and loop only when value gaps are addressable.
stages.coachInstruction: Clarify not a lender, customer receives funds, approvals/rates/terms vary, and funding is typically 2-10 business days before asking for payment or a firm next step.

branchRules: If the prospect has no financing, diagnose missed deals, price objections, reduced scope, lead source, and growth goals before solutioning.
If the prospect uses dealer-fee financing, diagnose fee percentage, financed volume, approval friction, pricing impact, and funding constraints before solutioning.
If the prospect asks price immediately, answer with annual plan range and bridge back to whether the economics make sense for their situation.
If the prospect cannot quantify pain, ask one math question and pause; do not continue into a full pitch.
If the prospect is poor-fit, explain why directly and avoid forcing a full sales presentation.
microAgreements: Does that sound like what is happening?
If we could solve that without dealer fees, would that be worth looking at?
So one extra job a month would more than cover this, right?
Does that process fit how you already sell jobs?
Is there any reason we should not get this set up today?

proof.claim: Enhancify is trusted by over 5,000 contractors.
proof.source: Enhancify public homepage and training docs.

proof.claim: Public site claims a 16% average increase in deal size and a 21% self-reported average increase in deals closed.
proof.source: Enhancify public homepage, By The Numbers section.

proof.claim: The platform supports financing up to $200,000, but actual loan amounts depend on lender and applicant creditworthiness.
proof.source: Enhancify public homepage and legal disclosures on public pages.

proof.claim: Training docs teach that most good-credit applicants often see more realistic personal-loan ranges around $10,000-$20,000, with excellent-credit applicants having access to larger amounts and better options.
proof.source: Setting Right Expectations of the Product.md.

whatItIs: A technology platform and financing marketplace, not a lender.
A co-branded online portal contractors can share by link, website, QR code, or mobile device.
A contractor portal with loan tracking, sales training, marketing tools, and partnership resources.
availableProducts: Secured loans in limited cases, often subprime-oriented and collateral-based.
0% introductory term credit card marketplace options for qualified applicants.
customerExperience: Homeowner/applicant completes a soft-pull pre-approval application.
Applicant reviews available options, then completes account setup and final approval with the selected lender.
All loan products ultimately require a hard inquiry before final approval and disbursement.
fundingExpectations: Personal loan funds typically arrive by ACH to the applicant between 2 and 10 business days when setup is completed promptly.
0% introductory credit cards usually arrive by mail in 7-10 business days.
Funding timelines are not controlled or guaranteed by Enhancify.

complianceGuardrails: Say Enhancify is a tech platform or marketplace, not a lender.
Do not say Enhancify controls APR, loan amounts, repayment terms, approvals, funding timelines, underwriting, or lender decisions.
Do not characterize 0% introductory term credit cards as 0% loans or as the same as direct-lender promotional loan products.
Clarify that soft-pull pre-approval is not final approval; final lender approval involves a hard inquiry.
Clarify that funds go to the applicant/customer, not directly to the contractor.
Do not guarantee funding before a job starts; explain typical 2-10 business day funding where applicable.
Do not promise best rates, broad approvals for every 550+ applicant, or large approval amounts for average credit profiles.
Describe the Funding Designation Agreement as a good-faith promise to use funds with the contractor, not a binding contract that forces fund usage.
Do not promise a 1% commission rate, large typical commissions, or specific commission outcomes unless policy is verified.

objections.responseStrategy: Answer directly with annual plan range, then bridge to ROI math: one recovered or upsized job can justify the subscription if their current pain is real.

objections.objection: I need to think about it.
objections.responseStrategy: Identify the no behind the no. Ask whether the concern is value, trust, timing, price, implementation, or partner approval; loop only against the real gap.

objections.objection: I need to talk to my spouse or partner.
objections.responseStrategy: Respect the stakeholder but test whether the prospect personally sees the value. If yes, set a tight decision path or use approved buyer's-remorse loop only when appropriate.

objections.objection: Does the money come to me or the customer?
objections.responseStrategy: Clarify that the applicant receives funds directly. Reframe that this lets newer/smaller contractors offer financing without direct-lender business requirements or dealer-fee model.

objections.objection: What if the customer takes the money and does not use me?
objections.responseStrategy: Explain the Funding Designation Agreement as a good-faith agreement and advise the contractor to use their own customer contract quickly; do not imply Enhancify can force fund usage.
Explain marketplace breadth and soft-pull pre-approval, then qualify expectations: approvals, amounts, rates, and terms depend on the applicant and lender.

objections.objection: How are you different from Hearth or direct lenders like GoodLeap, Synchrony, or Service Finance?
objections.responseStrategy: Contrast marketplace/no dealer-fee/subscription model with direct-lender/dealer-fee/specialized-loan models. Acknowledge tradeoffs: Enhancify avoids dealer fees but does not control lender terms or fund contractors directly.

objections.objection: I am too busy to set this up.
objections.responseStrategy: Tie implementation to their stated lost-job math, then recommend the plan level that matches support need, usually Professional Growth or Concierge if adoption risk is the issue.

competitors.name: Direct lenders such as GoodLeap, Synchrony, Service Finance
competitors.positioningNote: They can offer specialized loan products, rate sheets, buy-downs, and often contractor-direct funding, but commonly involve dealer fees, business requirements, and stricter credit boxes.

competitors.name: Hearth and other contractor financing marketplaces
competitors.positioningNote: Compare on pricing, lender network, support, commissions, contractor portal, marketing tools, and customer experience without overclaiming unsupported superiority.

competitors.name: Status quo cash/check/credit card only
competitors.positioningNote: The cost is lost jobs, reduced scope, delayed projects, and weaker marketing conversion when homeowners need payment flexibility.

voice.tone: sharp, enthusiastic, expert, direct, contractor-fluent, practical
phoneStyle: Ask one question at a time.
Use simple contractor language rather than fintech jargon.
Sound confident enough to ask for the business clearly.

sourceMetadata.sourcePolicy: Summarized structured facts only; no large raw script excerpts stored in repo.
